_README: Source of truth for the PM&A full-report figures: every plotted value is traceable to the full report and its cited primary sources via each entry's `source` field, so the figures are auditable against the report. Honesty rules: (1) no value that is not traceable to the report via its `source` field; (2) confidence intervals appear ONLY where the source reports them (Lancet PH 2025 and Wilker 2023); (3) incomparable metrics (per-5µg HR vs per-2µg HR vs dichotomized contrast vs road-proximity HR vs star-rating) are kept in SEPARATE panels, never one continuous axis; (4) the net message is 'more likely than not associated, heterogeneous' — the scorecard is deliberately mixed, not a clean sweep.

figure1_bradford_hill.title: Bradford Hill appraisal of the PM2.5–Alzheimer's evidence
figure1_bradford_hill.caption: Each consideration marked met (✓), partial (◐), or weak (✗). Source: §7 of the full report (Association versus causation). The mixed verdict — not a clean sweep — is the point: the evidence supports causation as plausible but not proven.

criteria.name: Temporality
criteria.note: Exposure precedes diagnosis by years to decades.
criteria.source: §7: "Temporality holds (exposure precedes diagnosis by years to decades)."

criteria.name: Consistency
criteria.note: Positive and significant on the largest pooling, but null in the Wilker overall estimate — concentrated in active-ascertainment studies.
criteria.source: §7: "Consistency is partial (positive and significant on the largest pooling, but null in the Wilker overall estimate — concentrated in active-ascertainment studies)."

criteria.name: Dose–response
criteria.note: Suggested by per-increment meta estimates and low-concentration Shi/Grande findings; Chen's gradient is road proximity (composite), so weaker concentration–response evidence than it first appears.
criteria.source: §7: "Dose-response is suggested by the per-increment meta-analytic estimates and the low-concentration findings of Shi [2] and Grande [3]; note Chen's gradient [4] is by road proximity (a composite exposure), not by PM2.5 concentration…"

criteria.name: Biological plausibility
criteria.note: Candidate mechanisms (§5) are coherent and supported by animal/in-vitro work.
criteria.source: §7: "Biological plausibility and coherence hold (the mechanisms in §5 align with the cardiovascular findings in §3)."

criteria.name: Coherence
criteria.note: Mechanistic picture coheres with the cardiovascular-mediation finding in SNAC-K.
criteria.source: §7: "Biological plausibility and coherence hold (the mechanisms in §5 align with the cardiovascular findings in §3)."

criteria.name: Experimental
criteria.note: Animal support exists [1,7], but human randomized trials are ethically impossible.
criteria.source: §7: "Experimental support exists in animals [1,7] but human trials are ethically impossible."

criteria.name: Specificity
criteria.note: PM is associated with many organs and outcomes, not dementia uniquely.
criteria.source: §7: "specificity is weak (PM is associated with many organs and outcomes, not dementia uniquely)"

criteria.name: Strength
criteria.source: §7: "the strength of association is moderate (hazard ratios typically 1.05–1.5), not large"

figure1_bradford_hill.bottom_line: On balance, the evidence supports causation as plausible but not proven.
figure1_bradford_hill.bottom_line_source: §7: "On balance, the evidence supports causation as plausible but not proven."

figure2_evidence_landscape.title: The PM2.5–dementia evidence is positive in direction but heterogeneous in design
figure2_evidence_landscape.caption: Estimates are grouped by exposure metric because they are NOT directly comparable: pooled hazard ratios use different per-µg increments, single-cohort estimates use different exposure contrasts, and one synthesis reports a star rating rather than a hazard ratio. Confidence intervals are shown only for the two studies that report them. Source: §4 evidence table; CIs from Lancet PH 2025 [5] and Wilker 2023 [9].
figure2_evidence_landscape.no_effect_note: HR = 1.0 means no association. A 95% CI that crosses 1.0 is not statistically significant.

panels.header: Pooled meta-analyses — hazard ratio (note the differing per-µg increments; the HR=1.0 null line is comparable, the magnitude is not)

rows.label: 2025 Lancet Planetary Health
rows.sublabel: meta-analysis, 21 studies, n≈24M for the PM2.5–dementia estimate · per 5 µg/m³
rows.source: §4 table, row '2025 Lancet PH [5]': "Pooled HR 1.08 per 5 µg/m³ (95% CI 1.02–1.14)"

rows.label: 2023 BMJ (Wilker)
rows.sublabel: meta-analysis, 14 PM2.5 studies · per 2 µg/m³ · overall null; positive only in active-ascertainment subset
rows.source: §4 table, row 'Wilker 2023 [9]': "Overall null (HR 1.04, 0.99–1.09); positive only in active-ascertainment subset"

panels.header: Single-cohort estimates — different exposure contrasts, no CI reported (NOT on the same scale as the pooled HRs or each other)

rows.label: WHIMS (Cacciottolo 2017)
rows.contrast: dichotomized: residing above vs. below the US standard (3-yr avg >12 µg/m³); stronger in APOE ε4/4
rows.source: §4 table, row 'Cacciottolo 2017 [1]': "~92% higher dementia risk; stronger in APOE ε4/4" (dichotomized above/below-standard contrast, not per-µg)

rows.label: Ontario (Chen 2017)
rows.contrast: residential proximity: living within 50 m of a major road (composite traffic exposure, not PM2.5 concentration)
rows.source: §4 table, row 'Chen 2017 [4]': "HR ≈ 1.07 at <50 m; distance gradient" (road proximity, composite exposure)

panels.header: Other syntheses — reported qualitatively or as a rating, not as a plottable hazard ratio

rows.label: US Medicare (Shi 2020)
rows.value_text: Significant positive association; detectable below current standards
rows.detail: no single per-µg point estimate carried into the report
rows.source: §4 table, row 'Shi 2020 [2]': "Higher hazard of first dementia hospitalization; effects below standards"

rows.label: SNAC-K (Grande 2020)
rows.value_text: Associated with dementia at low absolute levels; stroke the key intermediate (~49%), heart failure/IHD effect-modifiers
rows.detail: central finding is cerebrovascular intermediation, not a magnitude
rows.source: §4 table, row 'Grande 2020 [3]': "Associated with dementia; stroke the key intermediate (~49%); HF/IHD effect-modifiers"

rows.label: Burden-of-Proof (Nature Aging 2025)
rows.value_text: Dementia ★★ ("weak / inconsistent") · Alzheimer's ★★★ · vascular: not significant
rows.detail: deliberately conservative scoring method; star rating, not a hazard ratio
rows.source: §4 table, row 'Nature Aging BoP 2025 [12]': "Dementia 2★ ('weak/inconsistent'); Alzheimer's 3★; vascular n.s."

figure3_mechanism.title: Candidate mechanistic pathway (most direct evidence is animal / in-vitro; human mechanistic data remain limited)
figure3_mechanism.caption: A concept schematic of the pathways in §5, color-coded by the strongest evidence class behind each step. No quantities are implied. Source: §5 (Biological mechanisms) of the full report.

stages.label: Systemic circulation + olfactory translocation of ultrafine particles
stages.source: §5 Direct translocation: Oberdörster 2004 [10] (rodent olfactory route); Maher 2016 [11] (magnetite in human brain tissue)

stages.label: Neuroinflammation · oxidative stress · blood–brain-barrier disruption
stages.source: §5 Neuroinflammation / Oxidative stress / BBB disruption (animal & in-vitro)

stages.label: Accelerated amyloid-β / tau pathology + impaired clearance
Increased Alzheimer's / dementia risk
stages.source: §5–§7: plausible but not established human causation

modifier.label: APOE ε4 (and possibly TREM2 / complement variants) may modulate the response
